revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:西尾市立一色学びの館]]
'''Nishio City Isshiki Manabi-no-Yakata''' (西尾市立一色学びの館) is a {{ill|public facility|ja|公共施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/public facility|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 施設|1=facility|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q13226383}} located in {{ill|Isshiki, Aichi|en|Isshiki, Aichi|qid=Q1154666|lt=Isshiki Town|1=Isshiki, Aichi}}, {{ill|Nishio|en|Nishio|qid=Q317844|lt=Nishio City|1=Nishio}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It serves as the core facility of the {{ill|Isshiki Regional Cultural Plaza|ja|一色地域文化広場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isshiki Regional Cultural Plaza|1=Isshiki Community Culture Plaza|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109362532}}, functioning both as a {{ill|Library|en|Library|qid=Q7075|lt=library|1=Library}} and a {{ill|museum|ja|資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 博物館|1=Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q33506}}. It opened in November 1988 as the "Manabi-no-Yakata" of Isshiki Town, and was renamed to its current title on April 1, 2011, following the municipal merger. It operates as a branch of the {{ill|Nishio City Library|ja|西尾市立図書館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishio City Library|1=Nishio City Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11627740}}, and has been managed by MIC Group Co., Ltd. as the designated administrator since April 2022.
== History ==
=== Before Opening === In April 1971, the Isshiki Town Youth Employment Home opened in Isshiki Town, featuring a small library. During the period without a formal library, the {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Library|ja|愛知県図書館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Library|1=Aichi Prefectural Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11256818}} operated a mobile library named "Izumi-go," which served the town.
In 1978, Isshiki Town Middle School relocated to the Sakata area, paving the way for the construction of the {{ill|Isshiki Town Public Hall|ja|一色町公民館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isshiki Town Public Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} between 1980 and 1981. The public hall opened on November 1, 1981, with its library becoming publicly accessible in December the same year.
By 1986, the site including the public hall was designated as the Isshiki Regional Cultural Plaza, a joint development between Aichi Prefecture and Isshiki Town. The plaza is a comprehensive cultural area, covering approximately 27,600 square meters.
=== Manabi-no-Yakata (1988-2011) === Construction of the Manabi-no-Yakata began on July 8, 1987, and was completed on August 31, 1988. The facility officially opened on November 8, 1988. Unlike other local facilities, this project was entirely funded by Aichi Prefecture, with a total cost of approximately 588.53 million yen. Upon opening, the library in the public hall was closed.
The library expanded its services over the years, including storytelling events starting in 1989, and lending magazines and picture-story shows. In 1992, it received the Aichi Prefecture Comfortable Space Award for its accessibility and senior-friendly design.
=== Nishio City Isshiki Manabi-no-Yakata (2011-Present) === On April 1, 2011, following the merger of Nishio City with Isshiki, Kira, and Hazu towns, the facility was renamed Nishio City Isshiki Manabi-no-Yakata. Renovations between 2017 and 2018 expanded the library to the second floor, enhancing its capacity and facilities.
== Facilities ==
=== Library === - **1st Floor:** Focuses on children's books and lifestyle-related materials, with dedicated storytelling spaces and interactive zones. - **2nd Floor:** Houses general books, local history materials, reference books, young adult sections, and audiovisual materials.
=== Exhibition Hall === The exhibition hall spans three floors, showcasing: - The giant lanterns of the {{ill|Mikawa Isshiki Suwa Shrine|ja|三河一色諏訪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikawa Isshiki Suwa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社 (西尾市)|1=Suwa Shrine (Nishio)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q54153292}} festival. - Displays related to the {{ill|Toba Fire Festival|ja|鳥羽の火祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toba Fire Festival|qq=}}. - Exhibitions on local culture and history.
== Access == Accessible via the "Isshiki Town Public Hall East" bus stop on the {{ill|Friend Bus|ja|ふれんどバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Friend Bus|1=Friend bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11277378}}, a 3-minute walk from the stop.
== External Links == * [https://www.library.city.nishio.aichi.jp/contents/facility/annex1 Official Website] {{translated page|ja|西尾市立一色学びの館|version=99648229|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:西尾市立一色学びの館]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Isshiki Manabi No Yakata}} {{wikidata link|Q60986380}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Japan completed in 1988]] [[Category:Education in Nishio]] [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1988]] [[Category:Public Libraries by Japanese City]] [[Category:Public Libraries in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
